title: Protein Secretion Efficiency in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii with Fluorescent Protein
author: João itor Molino
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kfnctme

steps.title: Pippete 500 µL of TAP media...
steps.instruction: Pippete 500 µL of TAP media to each well of a 96 deep-well plate Pick single colonies with autoclavated pippete tips or teeth cleaning twigs. Add to the wells Cover plate with a gas exchangeable seal (Breathe-Easy®, Sigma-Aldrich®) Place plates on a rotary shaker (150 RPM) under constant illumination (50 µmol photons/m 2 s ), at 25 ºC for 7 days undetermined, TAP media per well Undetermined, growth temperature Low orbital shaker speed are sufficient for cell growth. Nevertheless, microtiter plate shakers can be used and enhance cell growth.

steps.title: Remove seal and transfer 100 µL...
steps.instruction: Remove seal and transfer 100 µL of culture to fluorescence reading in a clear bottom 96-well plate (Corning Costar, Tewksbury, MA, USA). Centrifuge the 96 deep-well plate at 3000xg for 10 min Transfer 100 uL of supernatant to fluorescence reading in a clear bottom 96-well plate (Corning Costar, Tewksbury, MA, USA). undetermined, sample size for each material 00:10:00, centrifugation time

steps.title: Read fluorescence in the bottom of...
steps.instruction: Read fluorescence in the bottom of the plates at fluorescent protein specific wavelenghts in a plate reader Ex: mCherry fluorescent protein Excitation: 575/9 nm Emission: 608/20 nm Gain: 200 Read fluorescence in the bottom of the plates at chlorophyll specific wavelenghts in a plate reader Ex: Chlorophyll Excitation: 440/9 nm Emission: 680/20 nm Gain: 100 *Compare results of the whole culture fluorescence and supernatant fluorescence to estimate secretion efficiency. **Check chlorophyll fluorescence to surveil for contaminations or cell growth failure.

steps.title: Read cell density by absorbance at...
steps.instruction: Read cell density by absorbance at 750 nm Cell density can be used to normalize protein fluorescence results, and obtain a estimate of each colony productivity.
